name: Pennsylvania "Dirt" Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 ounce semisweet chocolate (for topping, coarsely chopped)
2 pints chocolate sorbet or chocolate nonfat frozen yogurt
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare Chocolate Crust: Melt chocolate and butter in a 1 quart saucepan over low heat or microwave-safe bowl in the microwave. Stir in chocolate cookie crumbs until well mixed. Press crumb mixture firmly into bottom and side of greased 9 inch pie plate, making a raised edge around the rim of the plate. Freeze crust for 10 minutes.
2. Prepare Topping: Heat heavy cream, chocolate, and corn syrup in a 1 quart saucepan over low heat or microwave-safe bowl in the microwave until chocolate melts, stirring until smooth. Stir in vanilla and set aside to cool to room temperature.
3. Prepare Filling: Soften vanilla frozen yogurt at room temperature for 15 minutes. Scoop about 1/4 pint of vanilla frozen yogurt into 5 balls and place on a plate. Place balls in the freezer to harden. Scoop remaining vanilla frozen yogurt into chocolate cookie crumbs; spread to an even layer. Sprinkle with 2 tablespoons of chocolate crumbs. Freeze pie for 15 minutes.
4. Soften chocolate sorbet at room temperature for 15 minutes. Remove pie from the freezer. Scoop about 1/2 pint of chocolate sorbet into small dollops over vanilla layer, spreading evenly. Sprinkle with 2 tablespoons of crumbs. Scoop remaining 1 1/2 pints of chocolate sorbet into balls around top edge of pie. Place vanilla frozen yogurt balls in the center of the pie. Cover pie with plastic wrap and freeze for 15 minutes.
5. Drizzle cooled topping over pie and sprinkle with remaining tablespoon of crumbs. Cover pie loosely with plastic wrap and freeze until firm—4 hours or overnight. Cover and refrigerate remaining topping.
6. To serve, let pie stand for 15 minutes for easier cutting. Meanwhile, microwave remaining topping or reheat in a small saucepan just until spoonable. Cut pie into wedges and serve with remaining topping.
